etymology.description: 工作(工作) (gōngzuò, "work") + 人员(人員) (rényuán, "personnel") = working personnel; staff member. The word combines the concept of work and the people involved, signifying individuals whose role is defined by their occupational duties.
etymology.interpretation: 工作(工作) (gōngzuò, "work") refers to the act of performing tasks or labor. 人员(人員) (rényuán, "personnel") refers to a group of people in a specified capacity. Together, these morphemes create an intuitive concept of individuals who are engaged in tasks related to their occupational role, embodying the idea of staff members or working personnel.

examples.chinese: 图书馆的工作人员(圖書館的工作人員)很友好。
examples.pinyin: Túshūguǎn de gōngzuò rényuán hěn yǒuhǎo.
examples.english: The library staff are very friendly.

examples.chinese: 工作人员(工作人員)正在准备活动(正在準備活動)。
examples.english: The staff members are preparing for the event.

examples.chinese: 医院的工作人员(醫院的工作人員)每天工作很努力(每天工作很努力)。
examples.pinyin: Yīyuàn de gōngzuò rényuán měitiān gōngzuò hěn nǔlì.
examples.english: The hospital staff work hard every day.
